title: Getting started with deckglgeoarrow
text: Currently, following add*Layer functions available: addGeoarrowScatterplotLayer - points addGeoarrowPathLayer - lines addGeoarrowPolygonLayer - polygons layers accept one three data related inputs: data - R object packages wk, sf, geos terra file - local file path geoarrow geoparquet file url - remote URL pointing geoarrow geoparquet file addition, mainly developers, nanoarrow_array_streams also supported.

title: Getting started with deckglgeoarrow
text: style customisation, (Deck.gl) layers two types properties: render_options — constant properties across layer (use function renderOptions()) data_accessors — properties can vary across rows (use function dataAccessors()) Additionally, popup(s) tooltip(s) can defined various ways: TRUE - attributes feature displayed NULL/FALSE - popup/tooltip shown character vector column names - specified attribute columns shown Similar style cusomisation mentioned , popups tooltips can customised using popup_options via function popupOptions() tooltip_options via function tooltipOptions()

title: Getting started with deckglgeoarrow
text: Putting together, ’s example showing 1k points using addGeoarrowScatterplotLayer examples can found .
code: library(mapgl) library(deckglgeoarrow) library(wk)  style_openfreemap = 'https://tiles.openfreemap.org/styles/liberty'  n = 1e3  pts = data.frame(   id = 1:n   , geometry = xy(     x = runif(n, -180, 180)     , y = runif(n, -90, 90)     , crs = 4326   ) )  pts$radius = sample.int(15, nrow(pts), replace = TRUE) pts$fillColor = sample(hcl.colors(n, alpha = sample(seq(0, 1, length.out = n)))) pts$lineWidth = sample.int(5, nrow(pts), replace = TRUE) pts$lineColor = sample(   hcl.colors(n, alpha = sample(seq(0, 1, length.out = n)), palette = "inferno") )  maplibre(style = style_openfreemap) |>   addGeoArrowScatterplotLayer(     data = pts     , layer_id = "scatter"     , geom_column_name = "geometry"     , render_options = renderOptions(       beforeId = "water"     )     , data_accessors = dataAccessors(       getRadius = "radius"       , getFillColor = "fillColor"       , getLineWidth = "lineWidth"       , getLineColor = "lineColor"     )     , popup = TRUE     , popup_options = popupOptions(       anchor = "bottom-right"     )     , tooltip = "id"     , tooltip_options = tooltipOptions(       anchor = "top-left"       , closeOnMove = TRUE     )   ) |>   add_navigation_control(visualize_pitch = TRUE) |>   set_view(c(0, 0), 2) |>   add_globe_control() |>   add_layers_control(     layers = list("Scatter Layer" = generateDeckglLayerId(       layer_id = "scatter", beforeId = "water")     )   )

title: Use GeoArrow to Add Deck.gl Layers to a maplibregl/mapboxgl Map
text: Easy efficient rendering large geospatial data browser. deckglgeoarrow provides functionality efficiently visualise potentially large geospatial data Deck.gl layers top maplibre/mapbox map created package mapgl. quick efficient data transfer R memory browser, geoarrowWidget used. Layer creation done ‘JavaScript’ using geoarrow/deck.gl-geoarrow (see Features section details layer creation efficient).

title: Use GeoArrow to Add Deck.gl Layers to a maplibregl/mapboxgl Map
text: Currently, (MULTI)POINT, (MULTI)LINESTRING (MULTI)POLYGON features supported following layer functions: addGeoarrowScatterplotLayer point data addGeoarrowPathLayer linestring data addGeoarrowPolygonLayer polygon data Support layers, discrete global grid layers (S2, A5, H3), origin-destination layers (Arc, trips) point-cloud layers, among others, follow.

title: Use GeoArrow to Add Deck.gl Layers to a maplibregl/mapboxgl Map
text: Spatial classes following R packages supported (via data argument): wk sf geos terra

title: Use GeoArrow to Add Deck.gl Layers to a maplibregl/mapboxgl Map
text: following local remotely hosted files types supported (via file/url argument): GeoArrow GeoParquet Note, due restriction upstream JavaScript dependency, files native GeoArrow geometry encoding supported. WKB encoded geometries render! addition, nanoarrow array_streams (files) supported.

title: Use GeoArrow to Add Deck.gl Layers to a maplibregl/mapboxgl Map

title: Add Deck.gl PathLayer to a mapgl::maplibre() or mapgl::mapboxgl() map using blazing fast nanoarrow::write_nanoarrow() data transfer. — addGeoArrowPathLayer
text: map mapgl::maplibre() mapgl::mapboxgl() map add layer . data sf (MULTI)LINESTRING object. file valid local file path geoarrow geoparquet file added map. Ignored data supplied. url URL remotely hosted geoarrow geoparquet file added map. Ignored data file supplied. layer_id layer id. geom_column_name name geometry column sf object. inferred automatically one present. popup popup contructed? TRUE, create popup fromm available attributes feature. Can also character vector column names, case popup include columns. single character supplied, shown features. NULL (deafult) FALSE, popup shown. tooltip tooltip contructed? TRUE, create tooltip fromm available attributes feature. Can also character vector column names, case tooltip include columns. single character supplied, shown features. NULL (deafult) FALSE, tooltip shown. render_options list renderOptions data_accessors list dataAccessors popup_options list popupOptions tooltip_options list tooltipOptions ... can used pass additional props parameters deck.gl instance. See Details info.

title: Add Deck.gl PathLayer to a mapgl::maplibre() or mapgl::mapboxgl() map using blazing fast nanoarrow::write_nanoarrow() data transfer. — addGeoArrowPathLayer
text: modified map object added path layer.

title: Add Deck.gl PathLayer to a mapgl::maplibre() or mapgl::mapboxgl() map using blazing fast nanoarrow::write_nanoarrow() data transfer. — addGeoArrowPathLayer
text: ... can used pass additional props parameters deck.gl instance fine-tuning rendering behaviour. example, can pass list called parameters settings control GPU pipeline deck.gl instance. See https://luma.gl/docs/api-reference/core/parameters list available prarmeters. default, deck.gl layers passed maplibre() map drawn top existing ones. , however, possible inject layers existing maplibre (base) layer stack using render_options = renderOptions(beforeId = "<-existing-layer-id>") plot current layer underneath "<-existing-layer-id>". See example.

title: Deck.gl data accessors — dataAccessors
Deck.gl data accessors — dataAccessors
text: ... named accessors passed relevant deck.gl JavaScript Method.

title: Deck.gl data accessors — dataAccessors
text: List named accessors, possibly modified via ... argument.

title: Deck.gl data accessors — dataAccessors
text: Please refer relevant deck.gl documentation detailed description available layer functions. See Details list currently available accessors, defaults layer types apply . want map certain accessor data specific value, need add data provide column name respective data accessor. Currently, following accessors automatically set following defaults: getRadius = 1 (ScatterplotLayer) getColor = c(0, 0, 0, 255) (ScatterplotLayer, PathLayer) getFillColor = c(0, 0, 0, 255) (ScatterplotLayer, PolygonLayer) getLineColor = c(0, 0, 0, 255) (ScatterplotLayer, PolygonLayer) getLineWidth = 1 (ScatterplotLayer, PolygonLayer) getElevation = 1000 (PolygonLayer) getWidth = 1 (PathLayer) NOTE: accessors getPosition, getPath, getPolygon handled internally set! get*Color accessors accept either vector rgb() integers (0-255) hex color string (potentially also alpha) - see examples.

title: Deck.gl data accessors — dataAccessors
code: # default accessors dataAccessors() #> $getRadius #> NULL #>  #> $getColor #> NULL #>  #> $getFillColor #> NULL #>  #> $getLineColor #> NULL #>  #> $getLineWidth #> NULL #>  #> $getElevation #> NULL #>  #> $getWidth #> NULL #>   # modify selected accessors dataAccessors(   getFillColor = c(0, 0, 255, 130),   getLineColor = "#ff00ffaa" ) #> $getRadius #> NULL #>  #> $getColor #> NULL #>  #> $getFillColor #> [1]   0   0 255 130 #>  #> $getLineColor #> [1] "#ff00ffaa" #>  #> $getLineWidth #> NULL #>  #> $getElevation #> NULL #>  #> $getWidth #> NULL #>

what: deckglgeoarrow: Use 'GeoArrow' to Add 'Deck.gl' Layers to a 'maplibregl'/'mapboxgl' Map — deckglgeoarrow-package
title: deckglgeoarrow: Use 'GeoArrow' to Add 'Deck.gl' Layers to a 'maplibregl'/'mapboxgl' Map — deckglgeoarrow-package
text: Leverages high-performance 'GeoArrow' memory layout render potentially large 'Deck.gl' data layers 'maplibregl'/'mapboxgl' map created R package 'mapgl'. heavy lifting done 'JavaScript' side browser using 'deck.gl-geoarrow' (https://github.com/geoarrow/deck.gl-geoarrow/). Currently provides functions adding Scatterplot (points), Path (lines) Polygon (polygons) layers. support data classes R packages 'wk' 'sf'. addition, convenience functions styling data, tooltips popups, well layer management provided. Furthermore, remotely hosted 'GeoParquet' 'GeoArrow' files can visualised directly browser, without need first read R memory. styling instructions prepared user R transferred applied browser data arrives.

title: deckglgeoarrow: Use 'GeoArrow' to Add 'Deck.gl' Layers to a 'maplibregl'/'mapboxgl' Map — deckglgeoarrow-package

title: Options for popups and tooltips — popupOptions
text: popupOptions tooltipOptions passed PopupOptions object maplibregl Popup constructor. See maplibregl PopupOptions details. popupOptions tooltipOptions package differ respective defaults. : popupOptions anchor = "bottom" className = "deckglgeoarrow-popup" closeButton = TRUE closeOnClick = TRUE closeOnMove = FALSE focusAfterOpen = TRUE maxWidth = "none" offset = 0 subpixelPositioning = FALSE tooltipOptions anchor = "top-left" className = "deckglgeoarrow-tooltip" closeButton = FALSE closeOnClick = FALSE closeOnMove = FALSE focusAfterOpen = TRUE maxWidth = "none" offset = 0 subpixelPositioning = FALSE

title: Options for popups and tooltips — popupOptions
text: popupOptions(): options popups tooltipOptions(): options tooltips

title: Options for popups and tooltips — popupOptions
code: # default popupOptions() #> $anchor #> [1] "bottom" #>  #> $className #> [1] "deckglgeoarrow-popup" #>  #> $closeButton #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $closeOnClick #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $closeOnMove #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $focusAfterOpen #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $maxWidth #> [1] "none" #>  #> $offset #> [1] 0 #>  #> $subpixelPositioning #> [1] FALSE #>  tooltipOptions() #> $anchor #> [1] "top-left" #>  #> $className #> [1] "deckglgeoarrow-tooltip" #>  #> $closeButton #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $closeOnClick #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $closeOnMove #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $focusAfterOpen #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $maxWidth #> [1] "none" #>  #> $offset #> [1] 0 #>  #> $subpixelPositioning #> [1] FALSE #>   # modify selected options tooltipOptions(anchor = "bottom-right", className = "my-css-class-name") #> $anchor #> [1] "bottom-right" #>  #> $className #> [1] "my-css-class-name" #>  #> $closeButton #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $closeOnClick #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $closeOnMove #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $focusAfterOpen #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $maxWidth #> [1] "none" #>  #> $offset #> [1] 0 #>  #> $subpixelPositioning #> [1] FALSE #>

title: Deck.gl render options — renderOptions
Deck.gl render options — renderOptions
text: ... named options passed relevant deck.gl JavaScript Method.

title: Deck.gl render options — renderOptions
text: List named options, possibly modified via ... argument.

title: Deck.gl render options — renderOptions
text: Currently, following options automatically set following defaults: ScatterplotLayer radiusUnits = "pixels" radiusScale = 1 lineWidthUnits = "pixels" lineWidthScale = 1 stroked = TRUE filled = TRUE radiusMinPixels = 3 radiusMaxPixels = 15 lineWidthMinPixels = 0 lineWidthMaxPixels = 15 billboard = FALSE antialiasing = FALSE PathLayer widthUnits = "pixels" widthScale = 1 widthMinPixels = 1 widthMaxPixels = 5 capRounded = TRUE jointRounded = FALSE miterLimit = 4 billboard = FALSE PolygonLayer lineMiterLimit = 4 extruded = FALSE wireframe = TRUE elevationScale = 1 lineJointRounded = FALSE lineWidthUnits = "pixels" lineWidthScale = 1 stroked = TRUE filled = TRUE lineWidthMinPixels = 0 lineWidthMaxPixels = 15 layers beforeId = NULL zIndex = 1 zIndex can used set layers order multiple layers added map. Higher values plotted top lower values. ignored, beforeId supplied.

title: Deck.gl render options — renderOptions
code: # default settings renderOptions() #> $radiusUnits #> [1] "pixels" #>  #> $radiusScale #> [1] 1 #>  #> $lineWidthUnits #> [1] "pixels" #>  #> $lineWidthScale #> [1] 1 #>  #> $stroked #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $filled #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $radiusMinPixels #> [1] 3 #>  #> $radiusMaxPixels #> [1] 15 #>  #> $lineWidthMinPixels #> [1] 0 #>  #> $lineWidthMaxPixels #> [1] 15 #>  #> $billboard #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $antialiasing #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $extruded #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $wireframe #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $elevationScale #> [1] 1 #>  #> $lineJointRounded #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $lineMiterLimit #> [1] 4 #>  #> $widthUnits #> [1] "pixels" #>  #> $widthScale #> [1] 1 #>  #> $widthMinPixels #> [1] 1 #>  #> $widthMaxPixels #> [1] 5 #>  #> $capRounded #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $jointRounded #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $miterLimit #> [1] 4 #>  #> $beforeId #> NULL #>  #> $zIndex #> [1] 1 #>   # modify selected options renderOptions(radiusUnits = "meters", radiusScale = 10) #> $radiusUnits #> [1] "meters" #>  #> $radiusScale #> [1] 10 #>  #> $lineWidthUnits #> [1] "pixels" #>  #> $lineWidthScale #> [1] 1 #>  #> $stroked #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $filled #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $radiusMinPixels #> [1] 3 #>  #> $radiusMaxPixels #> [1] 15 #>  #> $lineWidthMinPixels #> [1] 0 #>  #> $lineWidthMaxPixels #> [1] 15 #>  #> $billboard #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $antialiasing #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $extruded #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $wireframe #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $elevationScale #> [1] 1 #>  #> $lineJointRounded #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $lineMiterLimit #> [1] 4 #>  #> $widthUnits #> [1] "pixels" #>  #> $widthScale #> [1] 1 #>  #> $widthMinPixels #> [1] 1 #>  #> $widthMaxPixels #> [1] 5 #>  #> $capRounded #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $jointRounded #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $miterLimit #> [1] 4 #>  #> $beforeId #> NULL #>  #> $zIndex #> [1] 1 #>
